ggchangepoint 0.2.0
  • Reference
    • Introduction to ggchangepoint
    • Comparing changepoint methods
  • News

Unified changepoint detection dispatcher

Source: R/detect.R
cpt_detect.Rd

Runs one or more changepoint detection methods on a sequence and returns a tidy ggcpt result object. This is the recommended entry point for most users.

Usage

cpt_detect(x, method = "pelt", change_in = "mean", penalty = "MBIC", ...)

Arguments

x

A numeric vector (the data series).

method

Detection method. One of "pelt", "binseg", "segneigh", "amoc", "fpop", "wbs", "wbs2", "not", "mosum", "idetect", "tguh", "smuce", "hsmuce", "np", "ecp", "kcp", "cpm", "robust", "decafs", "sn", "inspect", "sbs", "bcp", "bocpd", "strucchange", "segmented". Defaults to "pelt".

change_in

What to detect change in. One of "mean", "var", "meanvar", "slope", "distribution". Defaults to "mean".

penalty

Penalty type or value. Either a character string ("MBIC", "BIC", "AIC", "Hannan-Quinn") or a numeric penalty value. Defaults to "MBIC". Numeric penalties are honoured by the functional-pruning method (fpop); the changepoint-based methods expect one of the character options.

...

Additional arguments passed to the specific wrapper.

Value

A ggcpt object.

Examples

set.seed(2022)
x <- c(rnorm(100, 0, 1), rnorm(100, 10, 1))
result <- cpt_detect(x, method = "pelt", change_in = "mean")
result
#> ggcpt (changepoint detection result)
#>   Method:          pelt 
#>   Change in:       mean 
#>   Changepoints found: 1 
#>   CP convention:   left 
#>   Penalty:         MBIC = NA 
#>   Series length:   200 
#> 
#> Changepoints:
#> # A tibble: 1 × 2
#>      cp cp_value
#>   <int>    <dbl>
#> 1   100    0.467
ggplot2::autoplot(result)
